steps.title: Map the first half of the text
steps.content: The opening sentences present Clara Sprague Ellis's view that books for very young children should imitate how toddlers use language — a philosophy about what is most suitable for that audience.

steps.title: Map the second half of the text
steps.content: The remainder of the passage offers Goodnight Moon as the "most enduring example" of that idea and notes that its repetition, rhythm, and nonsense rhyme "speaks directly to Ellis's influence." So a specific book is then described as embodying the earlier philosophy.

steps.title: Eliminate the wrong choices
steps.content:
• "The text outlines a debate between two authors of children's literature and then traces how that debate shaped theories on early childhood education.": there is no debate between Ellis and Hart; Hart's book illustrates Ellis's idea. ✗
  • "The text summarizes an argument about how children's literature should be evaluated and then discusses a contrasting view on that subject.": the second half does not contrast with the first; it exemplifies it. ✗
  • "The text lists the literary characteristics that are common to many classics of children's literature and then indicates the narrative subjects that are most appropriate for young children.": only one book is discussed, not many classics, and no general list of characteristics is given. ✗
  • "The text presents a philosophy about what material is most suitable for children's literature and then describes a book influenced by that philosophy.": exactly matches Ellis's idea followed by Goodnight Moon. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
